mission: Define the canonical trust-zone boundary model and the exact gateway/BFF surface map so browser-reachable compute, tenant transfer, route ownership, context mutation scope, and recovery posture are explicit and validator-backed.

candidate_groups.label: Grant-scoped patient recovery
source_refs: blueprint/phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#5.4 Claim, secure-link, and embedded access algorithm

gateway_surfaces.rationale: Public web intake keeps a public-safe cache and a single intake mutation scope only.

source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#0.2 Continuity key and shell law
gateway_surfaces.rationale: Telephony capture stays explicit because session posture, ingress proof, and follow-up delivery differ from web entry.

gateway_surfaces.surfaceName: Secure-link recovery and claim resume gateway
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#5.4 Claim, secure-link, and embedded access algorithm
blueprint/phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#5.4 Claim, secure-link, and embedded access algorithm
gateway_surfaces.rationale: Grant-scoped recovery is narrower than the authenticated portal and must not widen into general patient authority.

source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#5.6 Booking, waitlist, hub, and pharmacy continuity algorithm
blueprint/phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#5.6 Booking, waitlist, hub, and pharmacy continuity algorithm
gateway_surfaces.rationale: Appointments stay separate because booking confirmation ambiguity and waitlist recovery are distinct from generic request flows.

source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#5.4 Claim, secure-link, and embedded access algorithm
gateway_surfaces.rationale: Embedded patient delivery remains explicit and watch-only because channel freeze and host capability law differ materially from browser mode.
Messages keep communication mutation and delivery-repair scope explicit instead of sharing a generic request surface.

gateway_surfaces.surfaceName: Patient health record and documents gateway
source_refs: phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#5.5A Patient record and results visualization algorithm
blueprint/phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#5.5A Patient record and results visualization algorithm
gateway_surfaces.rationale: Record and document views remain read-only and artifact-governed rather than inheriting wider portal command scope.
Home remains projection-only and may not inherit request, booking, or messaging mutation authority.
Request detail can launch several request-bound actions, so the mutation scope is explicit instead of hidden in one broad patient BFF.

gateway_surfaces.surfaceName: Assistive companion sidecar inside the owning task gateway
gateway_surfaces.routeFamilyName: /workspace/task/:taskId/more-info, /workspace/task/:taskId/decision, /workspace/approvals, /workspace/escalations, /workspace/changed, /workspace/search; Assistive evaluation, replay, monitoring, and release-control surfaces (derived)
gateway_surfaces.rationale: Assistive remains a bounded secondary surface so it cannot become a shadow standalone control plane.

gateway_surfaces.surfaceName: Clinical Workspace child review states gateway
gateway_surfaces.rationale: Child review states stay within the same staff shell but keep settlement-sensitive task mutations narrower than the root queue surface.

gateway_surfaces.surfaceName: Clinical Workspace queue and task canvas gateway
gateway_surfaces.rationale: The clinician workspace owns single-organisation review and command scope under one workspace trust envelope.
Practice operations reuses the workspace route family but must remain a separate audience variant because purpose-of-use and minimum-necessary mutation scope differ from clinician review.

source_refs: blueprint/forensic-audit-findings.md#Finding 100 - Support replay and observe return still had no authoritative restore gate
gateway_surfaces.rationale: Replay and observe stay explicit because they cross the assurance boundary and must preserve frozen restore posture.
Support ticket work stays tenant-delegated and masked instead of drifting into replay or platform-wide operations access.
Assisted capture is a support-owned variant because it can write recovery and intake state under a delegate scope without becoming the primary ticket workspace owner.

gateway_surfaces.surfaceName: Hub case, alternatives, exception, and audit work gateway
gateway_surfaces.rationale: Hub case work is split from queue browsing because cross-organisation alternatives and callback fallbacks carry active mutation authority.
Hub queue remains summary-biased and cross-organisation scoped without inheriting case-level booking mutation breadth.
The pharmacy surface keeps servicing-site partitioning and outcome reconciliation explicit instead of folding into generic staff workspace traffic.
The operations board is multi-tenant watch posture only and must not silently inherit intervention authority.

gateway_surfaces.surfaceName: Operations investigation and intervention drill-down gateway
gateway_surfaces.rationale: Intervention drill-down is split from the board because live control, restore, and handoff posture differ from watch-only diagnostics.
Governance stays distinct from operations because scope tuple, blast-radius declaration, and release watch posture are stricter and platform-scoped.

split_decisions.summary: Public patient web and telephony intake remain separate because session evidence and capture-channel recovery posture differ.
Grant-scoped recovery cannot merge into the authenticated patient portal because route intent and tenant scope are materially narrower.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#5.4 Claim, secure-link, and embedded access algorithm

split_decisions.summary: Patient home remains projection-only while request detail can launch request-bound mutations across several domains.
Appointments keep booking and external-confirmation posture distinct from general request detail.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#5.6 Booking, waitlist, hub, and pharmacy continuity algorithm

split_decisions.summary: Messages keep communication reply and callback-repair authority explicit instead of hiding it inside the general request surface.
Record and artifact viewing stays read-only and parity-governed, so it cannot merge into the general patient portal home.
source_refs: blueprint/phase-0-the-foundation-protocol.md#5.5A Patient record and results visualization algorithm

split_decisions.summary: Embedded patient delivery stays explicit because channel-freeze and host-capability posture differ from browser mode.
Clinical and practice-operations work reuse one route family but stay as separate audience variants with different minimum-necessary mutation scope.
Hub queue and hub case management stay separate because active cross-organisation mutation is legal only in the case surface.
Support replay crosses the assurance boundary and cannot share the primary support ticket surface.
Support-assisted capture keeps a separate support-owned ingress and recovery contract while reusing the main ticket route family.
Operations drill-down is split from the board because intervention and restore controls carry stronger authority than watch-only diagnostics.
Governance remains a separate control-plane shell because watch-tuple, blast-radius, and approval-freeze posture differ from operations work.
Assistive remains a bounded secondary surface inside the owning staff shell rather than a standalone published BFF.
